response.justification: Aisha has a broad and long software career, but the overwhelming majority of her work is in TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies. Her C++ exposure is limited to a single 6-month role at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no evidence of STL mastery, concurrent programming, or large-scale C++ architecture. The role demands deep C++ expertise combined with industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443), IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and experience in automation or embedded systems — none of which are meaningfully present in this resume.
strengths: High-performance, low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and concurrency primitives
Event-driven architecture and REST API experience demonstrated across multiple roles and the GISCollective open-source platform
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, dojos, and conference talks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal — limited to 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015; no evidence of STL, multi-threading, or modern C++ architecture at scale
No industrial cybersecurity knowledge: IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any of the required industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) are absent from the resume
Career trajectory is firmly web and cloud-native (TypeScript/Node.js/React/AWS); pivoting to on-premises industrial C++ platform development would represent a full technology stack change
